summaries.132551: Confirmed reproducible on stable 3.10.6 and master with a minimal TextField widget. Engine PR #44779 (autocorrection fix) partially improved the IME position but did not fully resolve the offset issue. No further fix landed; issue remains open.
summaries.93288: Confirmed reproducible on both stable 2.5.3 and master across iOS simulator and Android emulator. Comments only add reproduction confirmation with flutter doctor output; no root cause or fix discussed.
summaries.172635: Triager could not reproduce with the provided code sample on stable 3.32.7 or master using Apple Silicon. Reporter clarified the issue is specific to running the iOS app target on macOS via 'My Mac (Designed for iPad)' in Xcode, not a native macOS build. A second triager confirmed the behavior on master 3.33.0-1.0.pre-1146 using the same Mac-designed-for-iPad target.
summaries.107434: Root cause identified as Gboard version-specific behavior: reproducible only with Gboard 9.4, not with Gboard 8.0. Since Gboard 9.8 (released ~2020) no longer shows a floating autofill dialog at all, the issue is moot on modern Gboard versions. iOS was noted to always show autofill suggestions in a fixed position on the keyboard. Status unclear; question raised about whether it still warrants a fix given obsolete Gboard versions.
summaries.168693: Triager confirmed the behavior is specific to web and desktop platforms; on Android and iOS, holding a tap on the suffix icon also activates the cursor, which the triager noted is consistent behavior across those platforms. No fix or PR mentioned; currently open.
summaries.155919: Reporter was unable to produce a minimal reproducer but identified the trigger: when a Row child with Expanded wrapping a Text widget has its BoxConstraints width collapse to 0, the null assertion fires. The workaround is to wrap both Row children in Expanded. A related PR #155920 was filed but the issue was noted as still needing a fix.
summaries.107292: Confirmed on master 3.1.0 with an iPad and Apple Pencil. The webview_flutter plugin does not support Apple Pencil (Scribble/handwriting) input on iOS. A commenter also reported that flutter_inappwebview's InAppBrowser works with the pencil but InAppWebView does not. No fix or PR has landed; issue remains open and stalled.
summaries.155061: Root cause identified by @chunhtai: Flutter only has one UITextInput plugin active in the iOS embedding, so the second text field's semantics object is connected to an inactive UITextInput; it needs to activate itself when it receives a paste or copy command. Ownership transferred to team-text-input from team-accessibility. Issue was assigned to @Renzo-Olivares but the assignee was eventually auto-removed due to no activity.
summaries.153387: A fix was filed in PR #153398 but was not completed. The issue remains open with a partial patch label; a commenter encouraged others to pick up the PR as a starting point.
summaries.176913: Comments confirm the keyboard-opens-during-stylus-scribble issue is unexpected behavior; using the stylus should not show the software keyboard. The issue is related to the broader Android stylus support umbrella issue #175886 and the handwriting input implementation from #115607. A team comment escalated priority to P1 because keyboard appearing during stylus input is unexpected. No fix or PR identified; status is open.
summaries.123565: Issue is specific to Chromebook Android (Lenovo Duet); pressing the Control key generates a Num Lock key event alongside it. Confirmed not reproducible on standard Android phone or with a physical keyboard on a non-Chromebook device. A later comment reports the same issue on a Chromecast keyboard. No resolution or root cause confirmed; stalled waiting for Chromebook device access.
summaries.129664: Comments confirm the tab traversal edge behavior for element embedding remains broken specifically for the element-embedding case, while it works correctly for iframes. A team member confirmed the iframe fix exists and the element-embedding case needs its own implementation. On master, behavior improved due to multi-view work. No dedicated fix or PR filed; stalled but noted as improvement in progress.
summaries.122009: Root cause identified as incorrect keyboard frame data from UIKeyboardFrameEndUserInfoKey when the Flutter app is in a small Stage Manager window near the top of the screen. Comments link to specific engine checks at FlutterViewController.mm that fail to correctly determine whether the other app opened the keyboard. The issue is iOS 13.0–13.3 specific and related to the checks documented in a Google doc on keyboard inset cases.
summaries.82414: Confirmed on web and iOS (simulator); on Android the IME behavior differs such that intermediate composing characters aren't inserted into the text field. A secondary commenter reported RangeError crashes when programmatically inserting text via controller on web when selection indices are -1. No fix confirmed; stalled.
summaries.101985: Discussion clarified that contentPadding in InputDecoration already exists but shows padding during scroll (not the desired behavior for full-bleed multi-line fields). The proposal is for a proper scroll-aware content padding similar to BoxScrollView.padding. The issue was assigned to and then abandoned by @pedromassango; @justinmc also expressed interest. Currently unassigned and stalled.
summaries.117324: Engine PR #37719 made a best-effort fix for Stage Manager but this specific corner case (app near top of screen in Stage Manager) remains. The issue is in engine-side coordinate math in FlutterViewController.mm for the keyboard intersection calculation. Triager noted the issue may not be text-input-specific. Currently stalled.
summaries.40833: Root cause clarified: creating a TextEditingController inside build() resets selection/composing region info on every rebuild, triggering spurious onChanged callbacks during IME composition. The fix is to create the controller in initState. @LongCatIsLooong noted the docs should warn against this footgun; no significant documentation update has landed. Issue reframed as a doc improvement task.
summaries.153416: Confirmed reproducible on master 3.24.0-1.0.pre.575 and stable 3.22.3. The root cause is that when autofill replaces the manually-typed text, Flutter's autofill context still references the old (pre-replacement) text and triggers a spurious save prompt. Affects Android only per the reporter. Currently stalled with no PR.
summaries.174930: Root cause identified in comments: when using SelectionContainer.maybeOf(context) inside the same build method as SelectionArea, the context is an ancestor of SelectionArea and misses the registrar; wrapping RichText in a Builder that provides a descendant context fixes selectability. The documentation for SelectionArea and SelectableRegion should mention this RichText caveat. Issue assigned to @Renzo-Olivares; auto-assignment removal bot posted.
summaries.142588: Comments clarify that intercepting paste via PasteTextIntent and an Actions widget wrapping the TextField works on non-web platforms, but Ctrl/Cmd+V shortcuts are disabled on web (confirmed in the source). The right-click context menu also does not use intent-actions. No fix proposed for web paste interception; stalled.
summaries.86087: Comments confirm the caret is consistently thicker on macOS desktop and web compared to native macOS apps (e.g., Notes). The difference exists on both stable and master. No root cause or fix discussed; the issue description was updated to reflect the thickness discrepancy rather than padding.
summaries.11427: A production crash was reported with a RangeError in handlePaste when the text editing value has a -1 start offset, confirming the race condition exists. The code path was verified to still exist in later Flutter versions. No reproducible sample exists. Status is stalled with no fix; the issue remains open.
summaries.98742: Team confirmed simulating key events via RawKeyboard.handleRawKeyEvent only fires framework-side events and does not work for actual text input because input requires cooperation between the engine and framework. Suggested workaround for Windows is to use the win32 package's FFI to send real OS-level key events. A cross-platform simulation API was acknowledged as technically feasible but not prioritized.
summaries.164609: Triager confirmed the behavior on stable 3.29.0 and master; FocusTraversalGroup does not help. The issue is confirmed to be web-specific — on iOS and Android nextFocus() works as expected. Root cause not identified; workaround is to use FocusScope.of(context).requestFocus(node) explicitly. Currently stalled.
summaries.163946: Confirmed reproducible on Flutter master 3.30.0-1.0.pre.290 on web; works correctly on macOS and Windows desktop. The issue is web-only. A commenter linked this to #149979 as a possible shared root cause. Assigned to team-web as the fix likely lies in the web engine.
summaries.162298: Root cause on iOS is that Flutter engine's paste: method on the iOS embedder does not send the event back to the framework; the engine accesses the pasteboard directly and sends only the resulting text delta. A fix would require the iOS embedder to pipe the cut/copy/paste action type back to the framework as a method channel message, separate from the text delta. This is noted as potentially overlapping with custom system context menu button support. No PR or timeline; stalled.
summaries.177779: Triager reproduced a related but slightly different behavior: the dropdown menu closes immediately rather than staying at the wrong position in some test setups. Reporter confirmed the issue is in the mobile browser context (both DartPad and as a web app). Affects Android and iOS mobile browsers; specific reproduction requires the keyboard to close after the dropdown is opened. No fix; open for tracking.
summaries.167125: The crash originates from a 'sortedNodes.contains(focusedChild)' assertion in FocusTraversalPolicy._moveFocus during an insertTab: selector call. The reporter was directed to provide a reproducible sample but declined, citing that the framework's own error message instructed them to file the issue. No repro; issue is open for tracking with no fix.
summaries.95975: Discussion clarified why non-textfield widgets receive focus on mobile: accessibility features and hardware keyboard navigation require it. The consensus is that pressing the virtual keyboard's next/done button should move focus only to the next text-capable widget, not to buttons. No framework-level fix proposed; FocusNode.skipTraversal is the available (but imperfect) workaround.
summaries.154069: Confirmed on Flutter master 3.25.0-1.0.pre.132: pressing AltGr with a French layout on Windows generates both a Control Left and Alt Right key event instead of a single AltGr/altGraph event. The issue does not occur on macOS or web. Root cause is that Windows reports AltGr as Ctrl+Alt at the OS level, and Flutter does not collapse this into a single altGraph event.
summaries.179142: Comments clarify that the onDateSubmitted and onDateSaved callbacks only fire for valid DateTime values; when acceptEmptyDate: true and the field is cleared, null is not passed to the callbacks. Proposal is to change the callback signatures to ValueChanged<DateTime?>?. Confirmed reproducible on stable 3.38.3 and master. A commenter noted it may relate to the fact that calendar-based date pickers cannot be cleared.
summaries.143434: The issue is specific to the Pixel C emulator (API 29). On the device, TextInput receives a TextInputClient.onConnectionClosed message from the platform channel after the user taps, causing unfocus. The reporter confirmed they switched to a different emulator as a workaround. @LongCatIsLooong was CC'd for triage. Status unclear; possibly emulator-specific.
summaries.83986: Team confirmed SelectableText loses selection on setState because it rebuilds; the suggested alternative is to use SelectionArea, which was introduced to replace SelectableText for most use cases. The reporter confirmed they had already worked around the issue by using SelectionArea in their package. Issue effectively closed by the workaround of migrating to SelectionArea.
summaries.125125: Confirmed reproducible on master 3.10.0. The 1Password autofill colored box overlaying text on web was split out from #87735 as a separate tracking issue. The issue remains open and stalled with no progress comments beyond reproduction confirmation.
summaries.172741: Confirmed reproducible on both iPad simulator and iOS simulator on stable 3.32.7 and master 3.33.0-1.0.pre-1064. Triager also observed that holding the left arrow key moves the cursor to the right, not left. Native iOS (Messages app) does not exhibit the problem. Android is not affected. A contributor investigating a timer-based fix asked about timer usage in the style guide; a team member clarified timers are acceptable and suggested investigating whether other repeated key event scenarios are affected before proceeding.
summaries.169830: A triager measured the default CupertinoTextField height as 34px on iOS, below the HIG-recommended 44x44 minimum tap target. A native SwiftUI TextField on iOS 26 was measured at 22px, suggesting native is also small. Discussion narrowed to whether native iOS actually expands the touch target to 44px; no confirmation either way. No PR or fix; open.
summaries.179597: Comment links the issue to #42846 (general stylus button mapping). Triager cannot verify without the device. Issue is open and stalled.
summaries.183456: Confirmed reproducible on macOS. Root cause identified as Autocomplete failing to advance TAB focus when the next widget is disabled — the focus cycle gets trapped inside the autocomplete. The issue was rerouted from framework team to team-text-input. A PR #159455 was referenced. No fix landed; open.
summaries.128708: Discussed with @chunhtai who agreed the documentation of standalone vs. building-block widget semantics behavior is needed but has no strategy for a systematic rollout. A code sample was provided showing TextField merging its semantics node with a sibling RichText node (linked to existing issue #26336). Status is stalled; no actionable plan.
summaries.165734: Comments debate whether retaining field focus after keyboard dismissal via back-swipe is intentional; a workaround using WidgetsBindingObserver to detect keyboard closure was posted. The reporter clarified the real bug is that Flutter's TextInput connection is not notified of IME closure, so Flutter still thinks the keyboard is open. Confirmed on stable 3.29.2 and master 3.31.0; currently open with no fix.
summaries.154563: Issue is intermittent; triager could not always reproduce it, and it appears related to Android IME service state. The bug occurs specifically with TextFormField (not TextField) using keyboardType: TextInputType.none, and only on first focus with a physical keyboard before any other key is pressed. Suggested workaround from a commenter is to use TextField instead.
summaries.178945: Root cause traced by @LongCatIsLooong to SkParagraph's getRectsForRange returning incorrect bounding boxes when a parenthesis or '>' character precedes a newline in RTL text — the bounding box for the parenthesis is abnormally large due to font/mirroring interactions, causing the cursor to appear inside the glyph. Linked to existing issue #117140 and active PR #178258; an upstream engine/Skia fix is likely needed.
summaries.160662: Confirmed reproducible on M1 iPad Air running iOS 18.2 on master 3.28.0. After using an Apple Pencil and dismissing its menu, subsequent finger taps show the mini keyboard or pencil menu instead of the full keyboard. @hellohuanlin was CC'd. No fix or PR mentioned; open.
summaries.168765: Root cause identified: in SelectableRegionState._handleTouchLongPressEnd, the context menu is inserted into the Overlay before the selection handles, so handles render on top of the menu. Fix is to insert selection handles first so the context menu appears above them. A team member provided this diagnosis and offered to review PRs. No PR filed yet; open.
summaries.52746: Discussion raised that adding prefixIconError would logically also require suffixIconError, errorPrefixWidget, and errorSuffixWidget. A counter-proposal to have an ErrorInputDecoration class was raised. No PR or decision; stalled at design discussion.
summaries.184571: Root cause identified: CGEvent-injected Cmd+V keystrokes have the Command modifier flag present at the NSEvent level (performKeyEquivalent) but Flutter's internal keyboard processing loses the modifier state for synthetic events. A community commenter identified an additional pattern: posting flagsChanged events before the keyDown event (rather than only setting flags on the keyDown) may help, as some frameworks only update modifier state from flagsChanged transitions. Confirmed on Flutter 3.41.6. A workaround via performKeyEquivalent override in MainFlutterWindow.swift is documented in the issue body.
summaries.120680: Confirmed reproducible by a triager on master 3.8.0. Setting the `titled` attribute of `windowStyleMask` to `NO` in MainMenu.xib causes all text input to stop working on macOS. @cbracken was CC'd. An unrelated Android commenter mentioned a similar textfield issue on Go Router in release builds. No fix; stalled.
summaries.175834: A community contributor filed PR #177826 (not yet ready) to add an errorPadding or similar property. A workaround was documented using a prefix Padding widget combined with zero contentPadding. A related duplicate issue #181755 was also closed. Issue was briefly closed but appears to have been reopened; has partial patch label.
summaries.102647: Root cause analysis by @cbracken: the issue is in firstRectForRange in the iOS engine. Specifically, a zero-height markedRect combined with the code that only adjusts width (not height) when the caret rect is received instead of the composing rect results in a rect that positions the IME candidates view at the top of the character. Likely a race condition in the async composing rect update.
summaries.164282: Confirmed reproducible on stable 3.29.0 and master. Comments note that requestFocus() on a readOnly TextField doesn't scroll the view to the field. The reporter's use case is a DateField component (TextField with readOnly: true and onTap for DatePicker) that needs focus for form validation error scrolling. A commenter noted that the web HTML readonly attribute does allow focus, supporting the expectation that Flutter readOnly fields should too.
summaries.99330: Multiple users confirmed the bug causes modifier keys (Shift, Cmd, Ctrl) to remain stuck as pressed after the app loses and regains focus, breaking shortcuts and multi-select interactions. One user noted that modifier state should also be reported on mouse events (like HTML MouseEvent) so key state is accurate even when the app is not focused. Issue was downgraded from P1 to P2 due to no active work. A web-specific follow-up issue #162977 was filed. Affects Windows, Linux, macOS, and web.
summaries.157279: Triager confirmed the issue requires an ISO 102/105 layout keyboard to reproduce (additional key between Shift and letter key); no such device available for verification. @dkwingsmt was asked for input. Issue is stalled due to lack of device access.
summaries.184744: Root cause identified by a community member: recent Gboard versions started sending KEYCODE_SHIFT_LEFT key down/up events through InputConnection when the user taps the on-screen Shift key. Flutter's KeyEmbedderResponder synthesizes a corresponding right-shift key down event but not the key up event, leaving the framework believing a shift key is held down, which causes subsequent taps to be treated as shift-extend-selection. The trigger could be a Gboard update rather than a Flutter regression. Affects Gboard on Android; workarounds include pressing space or backspace to reset state.
summaries.159101: Confirmed reproducible in Firefox on both stable 3.24.5 and master; the bug does not occur in Chrome or Safari. The cursor does not visually update while an arrow key is held down in Firefox; it only jumps to the final position on key release. Shift+arrow does show a growing selection. Classified as a fidelity bug specific to Firefox.
summaries.175983: A commenter identified that explicitly setting textDirection: TextDirection.rtl on the SelectableText fixes the issue for purely Arabic text, but breaks mixed Arabic/English content. The underlying cause is likely incorrect line-wrapping interaction with bidi text selection, related to existing issue #39755.
summaries.177360: Comments confirm the issue is not exclusive to Chromecast; some Phillips and Samsung TV devices are also affected, and Google can reject Flutter TV apps during review because of it. A community member who tried to fix InputConnectionAdaptor.java was unsuccessful. The issue was discussed on the Flutter Discord (hacker-android channel). No fix; open.
summaries.95630: Confirmed on multiple older Android devices (API 25, 28) via Crashlytics production reports. The crash occurs in KeyEventManager._convertRawEventAndStore during a null-check dereference. No reproducible steps or device-specific pattern identified beyond production crash logs. @dkwingsmt was CC'd. Status: stalled.
summaries.99074: Clarified that on Linux, middle click pastes the current X11 selection (highlighted text), not the clipboard, and Shift+Insert pastes from the clipboard — these are distinct behaviors. On macOS there is no native middle-click paste. The framework tag is sufficient; engine involvement not needed since events are being received.
summaries.120435: Performance overlay shows red frames on first render and first keyboard open on the tested Xiaomi Mi Lite 11 5G. Issue may overlap with #120555 (iOS keyboard animation jank). On a Samsung S10 the jank was inconsistent and less noticeable. Commenter linked related issue #116836 (keyboard sync). Confirmed still reproducible on stable 3.32.5; issue remains open with no fix.
summaries.161576: Confirmed reproducible on stable 3.27.1 and master. The issue is distinct from #116889: the save prompt appears on the first call but not on subsequent calls unless credentials are cleared and re-entered. @LongCatIsLooong and @chunhtai were CC'd as authors of the related engine patch #57209. No fix; open.
